title: Lazy materialization: Method deserializeBinaryBulk of SerializationAggregateFunction does not support cases where rows_offset {} is non-zero
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
After upgrading from 24.x to 25.8.3.66, queries on views that finalize aggregation states of type:
- AggregateFunction(maxIf, DateTime64(3), UInt8)
- AggregateFunction(argMax, String, DateTime64(3))
start failing when an ORDER BY ... LIMIT ... OFFSET clause is added.
The error message:
`Code: 48. DB::Exception: Method deserializeBinaryBulk of SerializationAggregateFunction does not support cases where rows_offset 41 is non-zero (while reading column delivery_report_at) ... (NOT_IMPLEMENTED)`
This only occurs with LIMIT/OFFSET pushing the reader to a non‑zero row offset. Simple finalize queries work fine.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
- ClickHouse 25.6.8.10
- Not observed on 24.x series
### How to reproduce
```SQL
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS agg_test;
CREATE TABLE agg_test
(
id UInt32,
accepted_at DateTime64(3),
delivery_report_at AggregateFunction(maxIf, DateTime64(3), UInt8)
)
ENGINE = AggregatingMergeTree()
ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO agg_test
SELECT
number AS id,
toDateTime64('2025-09-24 00:00:00', 3) + number AS accepted_at,
maxIfState(toDateTime64('2025-09-24 00:00:00', 3) + number, (number % 2)) AS delivery_report_at
FROM numbers(100)
GROUP BY id, accepted_at;
-- Works:
SELECT id, finalizeAggregation(delivery_report_at) AS delivered_at
FROM agg_test
ORDER BY id ASC
LIMIT 5;
-- Fails with Code: 48
SELECT id, finalizeAggregation(delivery_report_at) AS delivered_at
FROM agg_test
ORDER BY accepted_at DESC
LIMIT 10 OFFSET 0;
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Code: 48. DB::Exception: Method deserializeBinaryBulk of SerializationAggregateFunction does not support cases where rows_offset 90 is non-zero: (while reading column delivery_report_at): (while reading from part /var/lib/clickhouse/store/193/19355362-a339-4eb4-ae52-48ad07191072/all_1_1_1/ in table qa_mgfn_hub.agg_test (19355362-a339-4eb4-ae52-48ad07191072) located on disk default of type local, from mark 0 with max_rows_to_read = 1, offset = 90). (NOT_IMPLEMENTED) (version 25.8.3.66 (official build))
### Additional context
Expected Behavior: Query should finalize and return results correctly regardless of row offset.
Actual Behavior: Query fails with deserializeBinaryBulk ... rows_offset != 0 exception.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE agg_test (id INT UNSIGNED, accepted_at DATETIME(3), delivery_report_at JSON);

INSERT INTO agg_test (id, accepted_at, delivery_report_at) SELECT number AS id, DATE_ADD('2025-09-24 00:00:00', INTERVAL number SECOND) AS accepted_at, JSON_OBJECT('value', MAX(CASE WHEN number % 2 = 1 THEN number END), 'timestamp', DATE_ADD('2025-09-24 00:00:00', INTERVAL number SECOND)) FROM (SELECT number FROM (SELECT @rownum := @rownum + 1 AS number FROM (SELECT 0 UNION SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3 UNION SELECT 4 UNION SELECT 5 UNION SELECT 6 UNION SELECT 7 UNION SELECT 8 UNION SELECT 9) AS a, (SELECT @rownum := -1) AS r) AS numbers) AS grouped GROUP BY id, accepted_at;
SELECT id, delivery_report_at FROM agg_test ORDER BY accepted_at ASC LIMIT 5;
SELECT id, delivery_report_at FROM agg_test ORDER BY accepted_at DESC LIMIT 10 OFFSET 5;
